title: AC Not Cooling | Tampa, FL
hero: Tampa latent load hides weak airflow: prove register mass flow and outdoor rejection before any charge work—wrong order buys compressors for dirty coils.
summary_30s: Not cooling means delivered BTU fell below load. Sequence: thermostat permission, indoor CFM, outdoor fan and coil rejection, refrigerant mass and metering, then pumping integrity. Humidity makes borderline airflow and charge failures look identical at the thermostat.

quick_checks.label: Thermostat permission
quick_checks.action: Confirm COOL, setpoint below room, blower runs, outdoor contactor pulls in on call.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Refrigerant work on a controls fault wastes money and hard-starts equipment.

quick_checks.label: Return and filter
quick_checks.action: Inspect filter; verify returns open; listen for return whistle.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Low evaporator pressure mimics undercharge; overcharge risks slugging.

quick_checks.label: Outdoor coil and fan
quick_checks.action: Disconnect power; inspect fins; restore and confirm strong condenser airflow.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: High head trips cause repeated hard starts in afternoon heat.

quick_checks.label: Indoor coil and drain
quick_checks.action: Sight coil loading and blower wheel; confirm condensate moves.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Wet coil kills sensible capacity and masks charge symptoms.

quick_checks.label: Electrical sanity
quick_checks.action: Note hot disconnect or lug discoloration; do not reset breakers repeatedly.
quick_checks.risk_if_ignored: Electrical faults escalate to winding damage and fire risk.

safe_diy: Replace clearly loaded filter when confirmed.
Rinse condenser gently after verified disconnect.
call_pro_if: Hissing, oil stain, or top-off history—leak locate and weigh-in.
High-pressure trips after cleaning—needs measured proof.
TXV hunting—needs gauges and safe recovery.

flow_explanations.A: No charge diagnosis without design mass flow at the coil.
flow_explanations.B: Low CFM drops evaporating pressure first; muggy supply follows in Tampa.
flow_explanations.C: No condenser activity is low-voltage, safety, or contactor territory.
flow_explanations.D: Returns, flex, MERV mismatch, dirty wheel—common mis-triage as low charge.
flow_explanations.E: High head from weak fan or plugged coil collapses net capacity.
flow_explanations.F: Weigh-in after leak repair is the ethical charge outcome.
flow_explanations.G: Weak pumping after verified airflow and charge is replace-or-major-repair.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Filter, duct, or dirty blower/coils reduce CFM.
Mass loss from rub points, schraders, coil, or line set.
top_causes.risk: Long-run low charge overheats compressor; oil return fails.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Debris, weak fan, bad cap, high ambient stack head.
top_causes.risk: Trips and hard starts damage contactors and windings.

top_causes.why_it_happens: TXV, distributor, or drier changes superheat profile.
top_causes.risk: Misread as dead compressor when unstable.

top_causes.why_it_happens: Valve or electrical loss after airflow and charge proven.
top_causes.risk: Hot shell and breakers are shock/fire hazards.

repair_matrix.fix: Coil/wheel clean; return/duct restore; filter correction
TXV or drier service with targets

how_system_works: Split AC moves heat via refrigerant between indoor absorption and outdoor rejection. Capacity needs mass flow, correct evaporating/condensing temps, and air across both coils.

repair_if: Leak accessible; airflow window met; repair under economic threshold

local_context: High dew points and long runtimes raise condensing pressure; debris and weak fans hurt more here than dry climates.
field_insight: Tight dry-bulb split with muggy house: check static and wheel before gauges.
cost_pressure: Attic August hours and recovery compliance stack labor.
decision_moment: High head with unknown indoor static—stop; prove airflow before adjusting charge.
cta_primary: Book measured diagnosis: static, coil proof, charge decision with targets.
cta_secondary: Trips, burn smell, or returning ice—shut off and call same day.
stop_diy: Do not vent refrigerant, defeat high-pressure controls, or throttle lines to tweak charge.
